data.title: Cardano (ADA) Price Prediction for This Week
data.description:
ADA was unable to hold above $1. Will bears take over?

Key Support levels: $0.90

Key Resistance levels: $1.31. Sellers Return as ADA Falls under $1

Early in the week, optimism was high that ADA can stay above $1 and challenge the resistance at $1.3. However, since Tuesday, sellers have returned and they managed to push the price under $1. At the time of this post, ADA is found at 92 cents.2. Momentum Reversal

The daily RSI is making lower highs as does the MACD histogram. This indicates weakness and could push Cardano in a deeper correction. The most important line of defense is found at 90 cents which is currently acting as a support. If this key level is lost, then ADA may drop to 80 cents next.3. Buy Volume Crashed

In November, the buy volume peaked. Since then, this cryptocurrency has been making lower highs. This is a bearish trend and the price shows it as well. Until buyers return, ADA may struggle to regain its bullish momentum. Hopefully, bulls will defend the price at 90 cents to avoid further losses.

data.title: UK Judge dismisses $770M Bitcoin landfill hard drive case
data.description:
James Howells, an IT engineer from Newport, Wales, has lost his legal battle to recover a hard drive with over $770 million worth of Bitcoin.

The hard drive holding over $770 million worth of Bitcoin BTCUSD mined in 2009, was mistakenly disposed of in a landfill, to which Howells is currently barred access by the Newport City Council due to its environmental permit.

In the latest development, Judge Keyser, the Circuit Commercial Judge for Wales, has thrown out Howells’ legal case seeking access to the Newport landfill to recover his hard drive.

Keyser said that the legal case had “no realistic prospect” of succeeding at a full trial, according to a Jan. 9 BBC report.

Since 2013, Howells has repeatedly requested the council’s permission to access the site, offering a share of the missing Bitcoin if the hard drive was found.

Howells’ case gained growing interest in 2024, amid Bitcoin’s historic rally to $100,000, which saw Bitcoin generate over 130% in yearly returns.

Cointelegraph has approached Howells for comment.

data.title: Deutsche Bank Cuts Price Target on Wolfspeed to $10 From $14, Keeps Hold Rating
data.description: Wolfspeed WOLF has an average rating of hold and mean price target of $13.23, according to analysts polled by FactSet.

data.title: Hongkong Chinese Shares Under Charge Get Released
data.description:
Hongkong Chinese 655 said the charges over certain issued shares by controlling shareholder, Hennessy Holdings, in favor of China CITIC Bank International, were released, a Thursday Hong Kong bourse filing said.

Hennessy Holdings had charged certain issued shares of the company in favor of the bank as security for certain revolving loan facilities given to Hennessy's parent company, Lippo 226.

After the release of the charge, Hennessy is interested in 1,477,715,492 shares, representing 73.95% of the company's issued shares, and none of the shares held by it faced any changes.

data.title: UBS Comments on The Canadian Dollar After Premier Resigns
data.description:
The resignation of Canadian Prime Minister Justin Trudeau on Monday didn't have a major effect on the Canadian dollar (CAD or loonie), noted UBS.

The bank doesn't expect major swings either as Canada heads into new parliamentary elections.

USDCAD has come down from highs along with the broader US dollar (USD) reversal, stated UBS. United States macroeconomic data and President-elect Donald Trump's post-inauguration actions will be in the driver's seat in the upcoming weeks.

The bank continues to see USDCAD moving lower in 2025 and expects it to revisit 1.41 by the end of March.

data.title: Market Chatter: D-Wave Quantum CEO Says Nvidia's Huang Wrong About Quantum Computers
data.description:
D-Wave Quantum QBTS Chief Executive Alan Baratz said Wednesday that Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang is "dead wrong" about how long it will take before quantum computers are commercially viable, CNBC reported.

"The reason he's wrong is that we at D-Wave are commercial today," Baratz told CNBC in an interview.

Huang said during an analyst Q&A session Tuesday that it will be 15 years at the earliest before "very useful quantum computers" are available, and could be as long as 30 years.

Baratz disagreed with Huang's assessment by saying that various companies are already using D-Wave Quantum's quantum computers. "Not 30 years from now, not 20 years from now, not 15 years from now," Baratz told CNBC. "But right now today."

Shares of D-Wave Quantum fell 36% during Wednesday's trading session, but rose 6.4% in after hours activity.

(Market Chatter news is derived from conversations with market professionals globally. This information is believed to be from reliable sources but may include rumor and speculation. Accuracy is not guaranteed.)

data.title: Market Chatter: 7-Eleven Owner Still Considering Options, Including Couche-Tard's Bid
data.description:
Seven & i Chief Financial Officer Yoshimichi Maruyama said Thursday that the company is still considering options, including Couche-Tard's ATD bid, Ito's proposal and a stand-alone growth strategy, The Wall Street Journal is reporting.

"The most important criterion is which choice will likely maximize corporate and shareholder value," Maruyama said, adding that the company hasn't seen all the details needed to make a decision yet. "We are working toward a shareholders' meeting in May...to decide what is best," he said.

This comes as the WSJ noted Seven & i Holdings reported a drop in quarterly net profit but promised to complete restructuring and seek further growth globally as it faced a takeover offer from the Canadian owner of Circle K.

Elsewhere, Bloomberg reported a special committee of Seven & i Holdings Co.'s board is still considering takeover proposals by Alimentation Couche-Tard Inc. and the founding Ito family, although it doesn't have enough information to evaluate them, chief financial officer Yoshimichi Maruyama said.

(Market Chatter news is derived from conversations with market professionals globally, an/or from other media sources. This information is believed to be from reliable sources but may include rumor and speculation. Accuracy is not guaranteed.)

data.title: 1 " Outperform" Penny Stock to Buy with 170% Upside Potential
data.description:
Surf Air Mobility (SRFM, Financial), one of the key players in electric aviation, has outlined its aspiration to secure long-term profitability. The company operates the largest regional air mobility platform in the U.S. and has announced its four-phase transformation strategy that will optimize operations and cut costs. A $50 million term loan positions the company to excel in the next phase of growth, and this plan is supported.

Not only does the firm make no progress on its airline operations, but it's also helping to develop electric aircraft and airline software using artificial intelligence. The goal is to decrease the environmental impact of regional air travel, enhance safety, and make the industry more profitable. Surf Air is the largest U.S. passenger operator of Cessna Caravans today.

Analysts give Surf Air an average price target of $12.50, a high that's nearly 170% upside premium than current trading prices at $4.61. Analysts rated the company as Outperform following the company's efforts to reach profitability by 2025 and undertake its transformational initiatives. Still, the upside potential is huge, but like virtually any new industry, the path to profitability is still muddled and strewn with difficulties.

data.title: Westshore Terminals Reports 2024 Volume Loaded
data.description:
Westshore Terminals Investment WTE overnight Wednesday said it loaded 26.8 million tonnes of product in 2024. The average loading rate charged was $13.76 per tonne.

Adverse weather conditions in the fourth quarter impacted operations, while unscheduled maintenance prevented loading at Berth 1 for seven days in mid-November, the company said. Late last month, Westshore experienced a fire on part of one of its four stacker-reclaimers and repair work will take approximately two months.

The company is forecasting loading volume of 26.5 million tonnes for 2025, at an average loading rate for the year of $13.55 per tonne.

data.title: ForexLive European FX news wrap: Dollar mostly firmer, pound woes continue
data.description:
Headlines:

  • The British Pound extends the selloff
  • Reminder: It is a pseudo market holiday in the US today
  • Weekly update on interest rate expectations
  • Eurozone November retail sales +0.1% vs +0.4% m/m expected
  • Germany November trade balance €19.7 billion vs €14.8 billion expected
  • Germany November industrial production +1.5% vs +0.5% m/m expected
  • Bavaria December CPI +3.0% vs +2.4% y/y prior
  • US December Challenger layoffs 38.79k vs 57.73k prior
  • PIMCO remains positive on UK gilts despite selloff
  • BOJ raises assessment for 2 of Japan's 9 regions in latest quarterly report
  • Many Japanese firms see need to keep hiking wages, says BOJ

Markets:

  • JPY leads, GBP lags on the day
  • European equities mixed; S&P 500 futures down 0.2%
  • US 10-year yields down 3.4 bps to 4.658%
  • Gold up 0.4% to $2,673.22
  • WTI crude up 0.3% to $73.56
  • Bitcoin down 1.1% to $93,409

The dollar is holding firmer at the balance in European morning trade, only down against the yen on the day. Meanwhile, the major laggard is the pound as it continues to suffer in the early stages of the year.

Cable slumped to hit 1.2300 early on before falling further to a low of 1.2240 before recovering slightly to 1.2275 now. The pair is still down 0.7% on the day though.

Elsewhere, the dollar is holding slight gains despite lower bond yields. The slight bounce in bonds is helping to pin USD/JPY back down as it drops back under 158.00 to 157.70 currently.

In other markets, equities remain more tentative in general but European indices are continuing to hold modest gains to start the new year. As for commodities, gold continues to shine brightly as it creeps up above $2,670 to its highest in nearly four weeks. $2,700 next?

With it being a pseudo market holiday in the US, there won't be too much for traders to really work with. All the focus and attention instead now turns to the US jobs report tomorrow. So, that will be the key risk event for broader markets to round off the trading week.

data.title: Ivanhoe Mines Guidance Seen Soft After Strong End to 2024 — Market Talk
data.description:
1148 GMT - Ivanhoe managed a strong finish to 2024, though the Canadian miner's production guidance for the new year was lighter than expected, says Raymond James' Farooq Hamed. The analyst says 4Q production at Ivanhoe's Kamoa-Kakula operation in Congo was better than anticipated, driven by higher throughput with the phase 3 mill development. Still, he adds 2025 copper guidance included a wide range, with the midpoint lower than expected. Hamed suggests the conservative range reflects the potential for power availability issues to impact operations. Raymond James retains an outperform call on the shares, but trims its target by C$1 to C$24. Ivanhoe was last at C$16.92, up 27% over the last year. (robb.stewart@wsj.com)

1211 GMT - The online gambling industry will likely continue to grow strongly, particularly in the U.S., despite facing a period of transformation, Morgan Stanley analysts write in a note. Within the sector, Flutter's online growth will likely outperform peers, supported by U.S. margin progression and improved performance in other segments, they add. Lottomatica is also well-placed for online outperformance and growth in the attractive Italian market, they add. Meanwhile, Entain's share price performance will be dependent on continued execution under the new CEO, following a return to organic online growth and strength in its Brazilian market. Flutter shares closed Wednesday at $255.88. Entain and Lottomatica shares are down 2.2% at 663.60 pence, and 3.3% at 12.75 euros, respectively. (najat.kantouar@wsj.com)

1122 GMT - The U.S. Food and Drug Administration's request for a label warning about Guillain-Barre syndrome risk on GSK's respiratory syncytial virus shot Arexvy could negatively impact vaccine sales, Bryan Garnier Research analysts say in a note. While a causal link between vaccination and GBS-- a rapid-onset muscle weakness caused by the immune system-- hasn't been proven, seven GBS cases per million Arexvy doses were observed, according to the FDA. "We believe the observed GBS incidence very close to natural GBS incidence in the non-vaccinated population," the analysts say. Arexvy sales already declined double-digit in 2H of 2024, following the narrowed recommendation to a smaller age range and more at-risk patients, the analysts say. Shares fall 0.04% at 1359.50 pence.(helena.smolak@wsj.com)

0911 GMT - Shares in shipping companies are under pressure after U.S. dockworkers and shipping companies reached a tentative labor deal, averting a strike at major ports. A strike would normally see an increase in demand for space on container ships and raise freight rates, boosting the ship operators' earnings. Jefferies analysts note that freight rates have been boosted recently as retailers increase their stockpile of inventory amid uncertainty surrounding tariffs and the potential port strikes. "The supply/demand balance remains steady, but a soft patch could come later this year," Jefferies writes. A.P. Moller-Maersk shares fall 5.3% to 11,100 Danish kroner while Hapag-Lloyd shares fall 6.7% to 146.40 euros. (dominic.chopping@wsj.com)

0625 GMT - U.S. and Japanese stocks are more appealing over those in the euro area and emerging markets, Pictet Wealth Management says in its 2025 outlook. A stronger U.S. dollar, superior growth metrics, and the prospect of tax cuts and deregulation make U.S. risk assets more enticing, says Dong Chen, its chief Asia strategist. Japanese equities should be lifted by stronger GDP growth. Earnings growth forecasts in Japan look reasonable and valuations are close to their long-term average, he says. By contrast, tariff uncertainties, continued dollar strength and earnings expectations that look optimistic make Pictet cautious on emerging-market equities. (monica.gupta@wsj.com)

data.title: De La Rue Shares Jump on Revised Takeover Proposal
data.description:
By Ian Walker

De La Rue shares rose after the company said that British private-equity firm Disruptive Capital GP and Pension SuperFund Capital tabled an offer to buy the whole company at the same price as previously indicated for a 40% share.

Shares were up 12 pence, or 12%, at 114.50 pence in early afternoon European trading. They are currently up 32% over the past 12 months.

The British software firm and bank note printer said Thursday that the PSFC entities offered to buy the company for 125 pence a share, valuing it at 245.125 million pounds ($303 million). On Dec. 12, the company said it was in talks with the entities to buy a 40% share.

De La Rue said Thursday that any takeover would be conditional upon the previously announced sale of its authentication division to Crane NXT, Co.

On Oct. 14, it said that the company had agreed to sell the business to U.S. industrial technology company Crane NXT for an enterprise value of 300 million pounds.

De La Rue added Thursday that it was considering its options and that talks with other parties over the sale of its currency business continue to progress, while cautioning that there's no certainty any agreements will be concluded.

The PSFC entities have until Feb. 6 to either make a formal offer for the company or walk away under U.K. Takeover Panel rules.

Write to Ian Walker at ian.walker@wsj.com

data.title: Sterling, Gilt Weakness Could Stem from Badly-Received Budget — Market Talk
data.description: Weakness in sterling and the gilt market feels like an inevitable consequence of a badly-received budget, says Abrdn's Matthew Amis in a note. Jeremy Hunt started the trend of leaving very little fiscal headroom and Rachel Reeves continued that trend in October's budget, the investment manager says. "As gilt yields rose into year-end (admittedly mostly driven by news from the U.S.), the limited fiscal headroom has been eroded." Abrdn expects that Finance Minister Rachel Reeves will have broken her own newly drafted fiscal rules when the Office for Budget Responsability present their updated forecasts at the end of March. (emese.bartha@wsj.com)

data.title: Ryanair plans to fly full summer schedule from Tel Aviv
data.description:
Key points:
  • Expects most airlines to return to Israel by summer
  • Airline announces additional capacity in German regions
  • Says European consumers 'remarkably resilient'

Ryanair RYAI plans to operate a full schedule of flights from Tel Aviv this summer and is hopeful that Ben Gurion Airport will reopen its shuttered Terminal 1, senior executive Eddie Wilson said in an interview on Thursday.

Ryanair is one of a number of airlines to have withdrawn from Israel due to war in Gaza and Lebanon. It restarted flights from neighbouring Jordan in December.

"We rely on (European aviation regulator) EASA guidance ... but our view is that we will be back," said Wilson, chief executive of Ryanair DAC, the largest of five subsidiary airlines operated by the Ryanair Group.

"We've got a full schedule I think for Tel Aviv...so we will be back in there for the summer as I think most of the other airlines will be," Wilson said.

Asked about the reopening of Ben Gurion's Terminal 1, which is used by low-cost operators, Wilson said: "We would hope that they would take the sensible decision to open that."

Wilson, who was speaking in Berlin, said Ryanair planned to deploy two additional aircraft to regional airports in Germany this summer, offering 800,000 more seats.

But he said it would not reverse cuts of 1.8 million seats announced at bigger airports like Berlin, Hamburg and Leipzig due to high airport charges.

"We will grow in Germany - it is just a question of when. And if it is not under this government, then it is under the next government," Wilson said, adding that Ryanair's German traffic could double to 34 million passengers in the medium term if access costs are cut.

Wilson declined to comment on current trading ahead of results on Jan. 27, but described European consumers as "remarkably resilient".

data.title: Norway Exits Aker Solutions Stake
data.description:
Norway exited its ownership in Aker Solutions AKSO by selling 30,092,943 shares, or a 6.11% stake, in the Norwegian engineering company for 963 million Norwegian kroner.

The Norwegian Ministry of Trade, Industry, and Fisheries sold the shares at 32 kroner apiece through an accelerated bookbuilding offering, according to a Thursday release.

ABG Sundal Collier and DNB Bank's DNB Markets were the bookrunners in the offering.

data.title: Tadawul Shares End Week Flat; Saudi's Industrial Production Rises in November
data.description:
The Tadawul All Share Index closed muted at 0.07% in the green as investors welcomed the country's latest industrial production report for November 2024.

According to the Saudi Exchange, the bourse closed Thursday trading with 12,097.75 points, with 96 stocks closing higher and 133 closing lower. The session was led by Nice One Beauty Digital Marketing (SASE:4193) at 7.69% higher, while Salama Cooperative Insurance (SASE:8050) saw the biggest loss at 5.88% lower.

According to the General Authority for Statistics, industrial production rose 3.4% year over year in November 2024, mainly due to an increase in non-oil activities in the country.

"Compared to November of the previous year, the sub-index of manufacturing activity increased by 7.2%, supported by an increase in the manufacture of coke and refined petroleum products which increased by 17.6%, and the manufacture of chemicals and chemical products, and the manufacture of food products, which increased by 1.6% and 1.5% respectively," the statistics agency said in its report.

Saudi Arabian Oil (SASE:2222), d/b/a Saudi Aramco, reportedly trimmed its prices for liquefied petroleum gas products in January. The state-owned oil and gas giant decreased its selling prices for a metric ton of propane to $625 and $615 a ton for butane. Reuters reported, citing traders. The slashed prices were due to a weak demand for the products. Aramco shares declined 0.18% at closing.

Meanwhile, Leaf Global Environmental Services (SASE:9597) shares closed 9.67% higher as it secured a contract worth 3.6 million Saudi riyals from Modern Building Leaders. The deal covers the delivery of environmental impact assessments and compliance services for Jeddah's Al Arbaeen lagoon rehabilitation marine works.

data.title: US Companies Cut the Fewest Jobs in 5 Months
data.description:
US employers announced 38,792 job cuts in December 2024, the least in five months, compared to 57,727 in November and 34,817 a year earlier.

Considering full 2024, employers announced 761,358 job cuts, the most since 2020, and up 5.5% from 721,677 announced in 2023.

Except for 2020, it is the highest total since 2009. “Companies underwent extraordinary change in 2024 due to rapid technological advancement and shifting economic conditions.

Most employers are anticipating additional uncertainty with the upcoming administration, which is leading to slower hiring and more layoffs in the short term from various sectors,” said Andrew Challenger, Senior Vice President of Challenger, Gray & Christmas, Inc.

data.title: Fires in LA Reach Hollywood as Economic Toll Nears $60 Billion. Here's the Latest on the California Blaze. — Barrons.com
data.description:
By George Glover

Out-of-control blazes are still raging in Los Angeles, with firefighters unable to contain one of southern California's worst natural disasters ever.

At least five people were dead early Thursday, with hundreds of thousands ordered to evacuate. Over 2,000 structures have been damaged, and over 100,000 people are without power.

Late Wednesday, a new blaze broke out in the Runyon Canyon hiking trail near to LA's iconic Hollywood sign. Fires fueled by winds have already devastated the Pacific Palisades neighborhood in the northwest of Los Angeles, burning down the homes of acting A-listers including When Harry Met Sally star Billy Crystal.

Private forecaster AccuWeather estimated late Wednesday that the total damage and economic loss from the windstorm and wildfires had already topped $50 billion. "This is a terrible disaster. We're just starting to get a clear look at the magnitude of the destruction and loss," the company's chief meteorologist Jonathan Porter said.

Electric utility company Southern California Edison had shut off power to about 136,000 customers late Wednesday, sending shares in its parent Edison International 10% lower.

Write to George Glover at george.glover@dowjones.com

This content was created by Barron's, which is operated by Dow Jones & Co. Barron's is published independently from Dow Jones Newswires and The Wall Street Journal.

data.title: Canada's Housing Market Ends Year on Mixed Note — Market Talk
data.description: Canada's housing market saw a mixed finish to 2024, with the strong fall market rally losing a lot of steam as resale transactions fell markedly in Calgary and Toronto between November and December. Royal Bank of Canada's Robert Hogue notes the drop in Toronto rolled back all advances made since September, though the soft patch wasn't seen across the country and a recovery is still on track in British Columbia's Vancouver and Fraser Valley and transactions remain near all-time highs in Edmonton, Alberta, despite edging lower in December. The economist believe further interest rate cuts by the Bank of Canada will stimulate homebuyer demand in 2025, which will keep the rally going in most markets even if strained affordability and federal cuts to immigration restrain the pace. (robb.stewart@wsj.com)

data.title: U.S. companies will need to increase their hedges against a stronger dollar in 2025, says Bank of America
data.description:
By Jamie Chisholm

The U.S. dollar is likely to keep strengthening this year, so American corporations should increase their hedges against a stronger buck, according to Bank of America.

"In 2024, U.S. corporates could afford to wait for the USD spot price to weaken before repatriating overseas earnings back to [the] U.S.," said Howard Du, G10 FX strategist at Bank of America in a note published Thursday.

However, he added: "In 2025, the macro backdrop calls for increased USD hedging vs. the past year, as consensus forecasts see USD spot ending 2025 stronger than 12-month forwards prices against many global currencies."

The U.S. dollar index DXY, which is a trade-weighted measure of the greenback against some other major currencies, this week rose to its highest level in more than two years, a move that can make U.S. imports cheaper but can also hit profits of America-based multinationals.

The S&P 500 index members on aggregate get 41.6% of their revenues outside the U.S., according to FactSet. If the dollar strengthens those sales can be worth less when translated back into bucks at the end of a quarter.

Consequently, corporate finance departments hedge against such an outcome, usually by purchasing a forward contract that locks in an exchange rate at a certain level for a period of time.

There is a cost to such hedging, so U.S. multinationals are unlikely to do it if they think they will benefit from a weakening greenback. That was the case in 2024, Du noted.

"As markets headed into 2024 a year ago, the consensus view was bearish for the USD. Market participants broadly expected the Fed to start the rate cutting cycle in 2024, which should act to further weaken the USD after the Q4 '23 selloff," he said.

"U.S. corporates could afford to leave foreign exposures unhedged heading into 2024, and the market had delivered," Du added.

But the macroeconomic backdrop is significantly different heading into 2025, reckons Du. Forecasters generally now see a stronger dollar following the re-election of Donald Trump, with the consensus notably bearish on the euro and Swiss franc, for example.

Global trade uncertainty and tariff headlines could drive the USD initially higher in 2025, Du noted, and the current level of implied volatility pricing would suggest any tariff risk premium is not yet fully priced-in.

"A combination of trend and the carry factors also discourages USD bears from fading the USD uptrend, unless a series of pivotal catalysts emerge," said Du. "Carry" refers to the trade whereby investors use money from a low interest rate country to invest in assets of a country with a higher rate.

In summary, Du said that given the likelihood of near-term USD spot uptrend continuation, and the favorable risk-reward of owning USD call options - conferring the right to buy the dollar at a particular price - U.S. corporates heading into 2025 should "hedge now, worry later."

Whether the USD overvaluation could start to normalize by mid-2025 would depend on the tariff outcomes and U.S. fiscal policy, he said.

-Jamie Chisholm

This content was created by MarketWatch, which is operated by Dow Jones & Co. MarketWatch is published independently from Dow Jones Newswires and The Wall Street Journal.

data.title: Cboe Global Markets Initiated at Market Perform by William Blair
Adentra Makes Leadership Changes Including COO Appointment
data.description:
Adentra ADEN overnight Wednesday reported changes in its leadership.

The company promoted Drew Dickinson to COO. Dickinson previously served as COO of Rugby Architectural Building Products prior to its acquisition by Adentra, and was most recently president of Adentra's U.S. Industrial business.

"As a member of our executive team Drew has leveraged his strategic, operational, and leadership expertise to deliver strong results," said Rob Brown, Adentra's president and CEO. "He has been instrumental in the company's growth and his expertise enhances our position to capitalize on the opportunities ahead."

Adentra also announced the retirement of Graham Wilson from the board. Rob Taylor will become chair.

Marie Robinson and Richard Roy were added to the board of directors.

Adentra's share price fell 3.2% on Wednesday to $35.00 on the TSX.

data.title: Main Street Capital Adds $123.4 Million in New, Increased Private Loan Commitments in Q4
data.description:
Main Street Capital MAIN said Thursday it added $123.4 million in new or increased commitments to its private loan portfolio during Q4.

The company said its private loan portfolio included total investments at cost of about $2.0 billion as of Dec. 31, with 95.4% of investments in first-lien debt and 4.6% in equity investments or other securities.

Additionally, Main Street said it funded total investments across its private loan portfolio with a cost basis of $108 million.
